wineries.name: King Family Vineyards
wineries.tastingInfo: Flights starting at $20/person; free for wine club members + 1 guest. Guided tours $75/person, Saturdays at 11am/1pm/3pm.
wineries.hours: Daily 10am–5:30pm; Wednesdays until 8:30pm; closed Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve/Day
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Full-size polo field with Sunday matches May–October; Blue Ridge Mountain foothill setting; gourmet cheeses, salamis, spreads, and hot French bread; covered veranda, expansive lawn, brick patio, stone fireplace
wineries.insiderTip: Visit on weekdays to avoid weekend bachelorette-party crowds. Tuesday mornings are practically empty.
wineries.groupPolicy: 10+ welcome, designated patio area; buses by appointment
wineries.foodDescription: Gourmet cheeses, salamis, spreads, hot French bread

wineries.name: Barboursville Vineyards
wineries.tastingInfo: Tastings available daily
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Thomas Jefferson-designed ruins on property; fine dining restaurant (Palladio); on-site lodging; founded 1976 by the Zonin family (Italian winemaking dynasty) — credited with kickstarting Virginia's modern wine industry
wineries.insiderTip: One of the few Virginia wineries with genuine historical gravitas. The ruins alone are worth the drive.
wineries.appeal: Wine club members, experienced tasters, history buffs

wineries.name: Blenheim Vineyards
wineries.address: Charlottesville, VA (20 min southeast of downtown)
wineries.tastingInfo: Tastings available
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Owned by Dave Matthews; modern, eco-friendly facility; hosts live music, art exhibitions, yoga classes. Founded 2000.
wineries.insiderTip: Celebrity-owned but not gimmicky — the wine is taken seriously. Good conversation starter for groups.

wineries.name: Pippin Hill Farm & Vineyards
wineries.tastingInfo: Tastings and food pairings available
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Garden-to-table food pairings; cooking classes; floral workshops; wine education events
wineries.insiderTip: Best for couples and foodies — the food program rivals the wine.

wineries.name: Veritas Vineyard & Winery
wineries.address: Afton, VA (western Albemarle County, near Blue Ridge)
wineries.tastingInfo: Tastings available; family-owned, 60+ acres of vines
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Sweeping Blue Ridge Mountain views; serious winemaking with a relaxed atmosphere

wineries.name: Eastwood Farm & Winery
wineries.address: Charlottesville, VA (5 miles from UVA and Monticello)
wineries.tastingInfo: Award-winning wines, housemade beers and ciders, wine slushies; women-owned, multi-generational, 77 acres
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Award-winning wines PLUS housemade beers and ciders; wine slushies available; live music events; dog-friendly and family-friendly
wineries.insiderTip: Something for everyone in the group — wine skeptics can try cider or beer.
wineries.foodDescription: Lunch and dinner menus by Chef Andrew Partridge

wineries.name: Merrie Mill Farm & Vineyard
wineries.tastingInfo: Guided tastings, vineyard safaris, curated food pairings; 5,000 sq ft tasting room
wineries.uniqueFeatures: "Most Instagrammable winery" — whimsical, eclectic decor; Friday Sundowners events; Sunday Blues & Jazz (May–November); curated food pairings
wineries.insiderTip: The visual showpiece stop — every group wants photos here.

wineries.name: Stinson Vineyards
wineries.tastingInfo: Tastings available; father/daughter team Scott Stinson and Rachel Stinson Vrooman
wineries.uniqueFeatures: "Garagiste" — built into an old three-car garage; Bordeaux and Loire Valley inspiration; panoramic Blue Ridge views; intimate scale; bed & breakfast on property
wineries.insiderTip: The small-batch, artisan stop — feels like visiting a friend's vineyard.

wineries.name: Early Mountain Vineyards
wineries.tastingInfo: Best-in-class wine and food experience; considered one of Virginia's top tasting room experiences
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Environmentally low-impact viticulture; best-in-class wine and food experience; considered one of Virginia's top tasting room experiences
wineries.insiderTip: The "best of Virginia wine" stop for serious tasters.

wineries.name: Southwest Mountains Vineyards
wineries.tastingInfo: Tastings available; opened Fall 2023
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Converted 1903 historic barn with soaring timber ceilings; set in Keswick horse country; winemaker Boela Gerber brings 22 years at South Africa's Groot Constantia
wineries.insiderTip: The "you haven't heard of it yet" stop — bragging rights for discovering something new.

wineries.name: Hazy Mountain Vineyard & Brewery
wineries.tastingInfo: Guided wine tastings (6 wines); 86 acres of vines
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Secret Supper Club events with chef-curated courses; brewery on-site; stone-fired pizzas, sandwiches, small plates
wineries.insiderTip: The "wine + beer + great food" stop for groups with mixed preferences.

wineries.name: Horton Vineyards
wineries.tastingInfo: Tastings available; founded 1989 — pioneers of Virginia wine
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Home of the original Virginia Viognier; grows Rkatsiteli (rare Georgian grape variety); skin-contact "Wildkat" wine; pioneers of Virginia wine since 1989
wineries.insiderTip: The history-and-adventure stop — unusual grape varieties you won't find elsewhere.

wineries.name: Afton Mountain Vineyards
wineries.tastingInfo: Tastings available; one of Virginia's first farm wineries — vines planted in the 1970s
wineries.uniqueFeatures: 360-degree mountain views; one of Virginia's first farm wineries with vines planted in the 1970s
wineries.insiderTip: Old-guard Virginia winery with a stunning panoramic setting.

wineries.name: Glass House Winery
wineries.tastingInfo: Boutique tastings available; estate-grown wines
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Boutique winery with a tropical conservatory; estate-grown wines
wineries.insiderTip: Quirky and intimate — the unexpected stop.

wineries.name: Mount Ida Reserve
wineries.tastingInfo: Wine flights and beers; open late hours; 5,000-acre working farm, 27 acres of vineyards
wineries.uniqueFeatures: 5,000-acre working farm; 27 acres of vineyards; farm-to-glass ethos; beef raised on the farm; wine and beer available
wineries.insiderTip: Farm-scale experience — feels like entering a different world.
wineries.foodDescription: Full food menu; beef raised on the farm

wineries.name: Upper Shirley Vineyards
wineries.address: Charles City, VA (along the James River)
wineries.tastingInfo: Reserved seating with tableside service
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Perched above a bend in the James River; reserved seating with tableside service; covered porch with river views; seasonal food menu; wedding venue
wineries.insiderTip: The "special occasion" winery closest to Richmond — river views are unmatched.

wineries.name: James River Cellars
wineries.tastingInfo: $5 for 13–15 varieties
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Family-friendly events; closest quality winery to central Richmond; affordable tastings; one of only 3 Virginia wineries growing Gewürztraminer
wineries.insiderTip: The "we don't even need a limo for this one" local gem — but makes an easy first or last stop.

wineries.name: New Kent Winery
wineries.tastingInfo: 10 hand-crafted wines; Talleysville Brewing on-site (12 brews on tap)
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Talleysville Brewing Company on-site (12 brews on tap, shared tasting room); happy hour specials; wedding venue
wineries.insiderTip: Wine + craft beer in one stop for groups who want options.
wineries.appeal: Wine and beer lovers, mixed groups

wineries.name: Saudé Creek Vineyards
wineries.tastingInfo: 14 varieties available
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Scenic Pamunkey River location; wrap-around porch; relaxed country atmosphere
wineries.insiderTip: Peaceful river setting — the "unplug and breathe" stop.

wineries.name: Jolene Family Winery
wineries.address: Richmond area (closest to Richmond Arts District)
wineries.tastingInfo: Tastings available; Lakeside Lounge tents for up to 8 people
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Lakeside location; stone patio; chic tasting room; covered greenhouse; Lakeside Lounge tents; food trucks; RVA Cheese Girl partnerships
wineries.insiderTip: Urban-adjacent — feels like a city escape without the drive.
wineries.appeal: Urban escape, casual groups, date nights
wineries.foodDescription: Food trucks and RVA Cheese Girl partnerships

wineries.name: Ashton Creek Vineyard
wineries.tastingInfo: Tastings with food/wine pairings available
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Kitchen on-site for food/wine pairings
wineries.insiderTip: South-side option for groups coming from that direction.

wineries.name: 7 Lady Vineyards at Dover Hall
wineries.tastingInfo: Pop-up tastings; wine dinners; wine club available
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Virginia-grown wines plus varietally-correct international styles; pop-up tastings; wine dinners; wine club; weddings and events
wineries.insiderTip: West-end option with a polished events vibe.

wineries.name: Fifty-Third Winery
wineries.tastingInfo: Self-guided flights, traditional tastings, private tastings; Heart of Virginia Wine Trail member
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Heart of Virginia Wine Trail member; self-guided flights, traditional tastings, private tastings available
wineries.insiderTip: Midway point between Richmond and Charlottesville — flexible routing stop.

wineries.name: Cooper Vineyards
wineries.tastingInfo: Tasting room voted Virginia's best (2012/2013); two-story glass walls overlooking vineyard
signatureWines: Noche (sweet red dessert wine, Norton blend, French oak aged)
wineries.uniqueFeatures: Voted Virginia's best tasting room (2012/2013); two-story glass walls overlooking vineyard; environmentally friendly construction; Friday live music with food trucks
wineries.insiderTip: Architecture lovers and dessert wine fans — the tasting room itself is a destination.

stops.note: Start here for the setting and polo-field views
Lunch break at a Charlottesville restaurant or winery food option
25 min drive, end with the Instagram stop
Mountain views and award-winning Petit Verdot
10 min drive, pizza and beer for lunch
15 min drive, close the day strong (or King Family)

itineraries.name: Richmond Quick Escape
itineraries.bestFor: Shorter timeframe, local exploration, weeknight groups

stops.note: 30 min drive, end with James River sunset

itineraries.name: The Group Celebration
itineraries.bestFor: Bachelorette parties, birthdays, corporate groups (8–20 people)

stops.note: Wine, beer, cider — something for everyone
10 min drive, Dave Matthews connection is a crowd-pleaser
20 min drive, polo field tailgating if Sunday

itineraries.name: The Connoisseur's Trail
itineraries.bestFor: Wine club members, experienced tasters, anniversary trips

stops.note: Start with the Octagon and Jefferson ruins
15 min drive, rare varietals and Virginia wine history
20 min drive, best tasting room experience in Virginia
25 min drive, newest winery, South African winemaker

seasons.spring: April–May: Vineyard bloom, mild weather, lighter crowds
seasons.summer: June–August: Peak season, polo matches, outdoor concerts, food truck festivals
seasons.fall: September–November: Harvest season, crush events, foliage — most popular
seasons.winter: December–February: Cozy fireplaces, barrel tastings, lowest crowds
